sections.paragraph:
πανοπλία, ας, ἡ (πᾶς, ὅπλον; Hdt., Aristoph. et al.; Polyb. 3, 62, 5; 4, 56, 3; Diod S 20, 84, 3; ins, LXX)

① the complete equipment of a heavy-armed soldier, full armor (2 Km 2:21; 2 Macc 3:25; Jos., Bell. 2, 649, Ant. 7, 104; 20, 110) Lk 11:22 (on vss. 21f cp. 4 Macc 3:12 A τὰς πανοπλίας καθωπλίσαντο [s. SLegasse, NovT 5, ’62, 5–9]).

② IPol 6:2 marks a transition in the direction of a nonliteral mng.; here endurance is compared with πανοπλία in a context that uses many concepts fr. the life of a soldier, and specif. mentions separate parts of his equipment. Purely metaphoric is πανοπλία τοῦ θεοῦ full armor of God Eph 6:11, 13 (fig. use of π. also Wsd 5:17; Sir 46:6; Philo, Somn. 1, 103; 108; other reff. Straub 91f). On ἀναλαβεῖν τὴν πανοπλίαν vs. 13 s. ἀναλαμβάνω 2.—On a Christian’s ‘military service’ and ‘warfare’ s. AHarnack, Militia Christi 1905; MMeinertz, D. Ap. Pls und d. Kampf: Internat. Monatsschr. 11, 1917, 1115–50; MDibelius, Hdb. exc. on Eph 6:10 and 1 Ti 1:18; AVitti, Militum Christi Regis arma iuxta S. Paulum: Verbum Domini 7, 1927, 310–18; Cumont3 ’31, xiif; 207f; HEmonds: Hlg. Überliefg. (ed. by OCasel) ’38, 21–50 (anc. philos.); EFavier, L’armure du soldat de dieu d’après s. Paul ’38; CBond, Winning w. God (on Eph 6:10–18) ’40.—B. 1398. M-M. TW.

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

NovT NovT = Novum Testamentum (periodical)—List 6

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

  1. fr. = from

specif. specif. = specifically

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Straub Straub = WS., Die Bildersprache des Apostels Paulus—List 6

Hdb. Hdb. = Handbuch (in connection with a specific NT book, the ref. is to a commentary in the series Handbuch zum Neuen Testament, founded by HLietzmann)—List 6

exc. exc. = except

Cumont Cumont = FC., Die orientalische Religionen im römischen Heidentum—List 6

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition
  2. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 754
